RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 5yo mare who raced in two bumpers at Newcastle for Paul Robson, winning comfortably on AW then well beaten on turf; concedes a stone to some 3yos who have already shown promise under this code, on grass to boot.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 2,500,000gns yearling; sister to her yard's Arc winner Alpinista; only seventh of nine at Newbury (1m, soft) in sole 2yo start, having been described by her trainer beforehand as "more of a long-term prospect"; interesting on reappearance, with improvement seeming on the cards.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Brings solid claims having made the frame in both starts, keeping-on second at Salisbury (1m2f, good) latest; sets the form standard to boot and duly commands respect upped further in trip (bred to be suited by 1m4f).

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Half-sister to several winners, including two smart ones, for her stable; finished third of four at Lingfield (1m2f, good) where she was the sole newcomer and showed signs of greenness; should take a step forward.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Rallied for second of four, albeit 12l behind the overwhelming market leader, at Ripon (1m3f, good) on seasonal debut; bred to do better still, being from a family that has served her connections well.

OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: His standout effort in three novice/maiden events was a 5l third at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) on his second start; not easy to weigh up on handicap debut but he's bred to be useful and his dam ran well in a Group 3 at this trip; needs a close look in the market.

Off the mark at the sixth attempt with a narrow win in a Wolverhampton handicap (1m4f, AW) last month; disappointing favourite at Ffos Las (1m4f, good to soft) last time but he still has potential and has shaped as though this new trip should be within range; in the mix.

Big improvement upped to 1m4f on handicap/seasonal debut when making all for an emphatic win at Southwell (1m4f, AW) last week; 4lb well in under a penalty for that success and he's open to more progress on this step up to 1m6f; should be hard to beat.
0-5 but she ran her best race so far when a rallying third in handicap here (11.5, soft) two weeks ago; shaped like a stayer in that race and she could be dangerous if she can build on that.

RaceName: Talasey Restricted Novice Stakes (For Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 40,000gns foal, £48,000 yearling; Oasis Dream half-brother to 7f AW winner Puntarelle (RPR 79), out of Listed-placed 1m2f scorer (103); sole newcomer in the field and it's his belated debut; market instructive.

RaceName: Talasey Restricted Novice Stakes (For Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Absent and gelded since finishing 4l second to the market leader at Carlisle (6f, soft) a year ago when attempting to follow up his Windsor success (6f, good); sets the form standard on reappearance and, assuming all is fine, still has potential to boot.

RaceName: Talasey Restricted Novice Stakes (For Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has kept on from rear in two races (6f/7f) over a year apart, showing a degree of promise; normally the type who should be more interesting in handicaps shortly but, on bare figures, has place possibilities in this field.

RaceName: Talasey Restricted Novice Stakes (For Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 4yo filly who is getting familiar with Doncaster but isn't particularly solid on her two efforts, both here (1m/6f); likely to find better opportunities in handicaps soon.

RaceName: Talasey Restricted Novice Stakes (For Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Twice-raced 3yo who brings the worst from; drops a furlong in trip.

RaceName: Talasey Restricted Novice Stakes (For Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: First foal of a 6f Listed winner; seemed to need the outing/experience in 5.7f contest at Bath, finishing seventh of 11; third best on bare figures in this field and is open to improvement; place claims.

RaceName: Talasey Restricted Novice Stakes (For Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Finished fifth of ten in 6f AW maiden at Kempton where she was the sole newcomer in the field and proved easy to back; ran to a meagre RPR but may improve with the experience under her belt.

RaceName: Talasey Restricted Novice Stakes (For Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Mrs Bv Sangster,Justin Casse & Dr J Berk
Spotlight: Never dangerous in 7f AW maiden at Kempton, having been weak in the betting, but may fare better with the outing under her belt; similar type to Elmfield.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: 9-1 for debut at Redcar (5f, good to firm) last month, when holding a prominent position before weakening; that form is modest and she needs lots of improvement upped in trip.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Home-bred by Twilight Son; showed ability on Wolverhampton debut (6f, AW) last month and she built on that when third at Pontefract (6f, good); was no match for the first two last time but she has a good pedigree and should continue to improve with experience.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: 20,000gns yearling; second foal; dam unraced sister to useful 6f 2yo/7f winner Maid A Million, half-sister to 7f Group 3 winner Dandhu, out of unraced half-sister to 1m Group 1 winner Rajeem; yard 15% with 2yos this season and she needs watching in market on debut; stablemate of Jazzy Blue.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: £21,000 yearling; fourth foal; half-sister to 5f AW winner Sweet Carolina (RPR 74); dam 7f-8.6f winner (including AW Listed; 103), half-sister to useful 1m2f-1m6f Flat and 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner Totalize, family of Juddmonte winner One So Wonderful; has speed and stamina in pedigree and market should guide.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: 32,000gns yearling; fifth foal; half-sister to winners Dark Thirty (6f/7f including 2yo; RPR 103) and Sandy Craic (7f; 73); dam Listed-placed 7f-1m2f winner (including 2yo; 95), out of 1m4f/1m5f winning half-sister to 5f Listed winner Emerald Peace; first 2yo runner for yard this year and she's probably best watched.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: 38,000gns sister to 6f winner Soul Love (including 2yo/AW/Listed; RPR 100), half-sister to Group-placed 7f/1m winner Excellent Believe (112); dam Listed-placed 5f 2yo winner (96), out of half-sister to dam of good miler Mohaather; looks the part on paper and she's an interesting newcomer.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Improved on her debut form when a rallying third at Chepstow (6f, good to soft) last month; found things tough at Epsom last time but that was in the Woodcote and she could resume her progress back in calmer waters here.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Ran green on her C&D debut last month but she left that form a long way behind when runner-up at Leicester (7f, good) 18 days ago; that was good effort under a prominent ride and she sets the standard on this drop back in trip; key player.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Has improved with each of her three runs and was beaten less than 2l when third of ten at Lingfield (6f, AW) this month; finished behind Zigazig Ah in that race but she's on the right path and is not ruled out.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Beaten 7l in two turf runs (6f/5f, good) last month with RPRs in the 50s on both occasions; has plenty to find on the figures and nurseries will be more suitable.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: 18,000gns foal, 7,500gns yearling; second foal; dam unraced half-sister to French 9.5f winner Point Of Fact, out of French 1m 2yo winner, is granddaughter of French Oaks/Moulin winner Nebraska Tornado; has fair target to aim at on debut and she could be a longer-term prospect.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: Made a low-key start over C&D last month but she took a major step forward when third at Lingfield (6f, AW) three weeks ago; that was a strong-finishing effort from off the pace and she's a player if she can build on that.

RaceName: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)
Spotlight: February foal; Rajasinghe sister to two winners including Giant (7f/1m AW including 2yo; RPR 90); well-backed into 7-2 at Lingfield (6f, AW) this month and she made a bold bid before finishing a close second of ten (Last Dandelion third); that was a promising start and she's strongly respected.
Ended last season with a win off just 1lb lower; has steadily found his form this term, creditable third over C&D most recently; possibilities, provided he takes well to first-time headgear.
Not at best last time but is normally consistent and may rebound; gained most recent success in last year's Ayr Bronze Cup (by a neck off 2lb lower) and is 1-1 at Doncaster.
Progressive over 7f this term until latest start when admittedly not disgraced; won well at Doncaster to initiate a May double and he looks a possible danger if coping with the return to 6f.
Gave impression something was possibly amiss last time, taking record for new yard to 0-4; interesting on best form, which includes a win over C&D and close second to Photosynthesis at Newmarket.
Tough sort whose form over C&D features a success last August and sound efforts in two of his last three outings; ties in with two of today's rivals on the latest one; solid chance.

OwnerName: D Caslin, P Wilcock & M Cantillon
Spotlight: Has posted duck eggs in big fields since his reappearance effort but this scenario may suit him better; performed well at Doncaster twice last term and he's 5lb below last winning mark.

OwnerName: Alfa Site Services & Alan Gove Racing
Spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who has a record of 2-4 since handicapping, most recently making all at Pontefract (6f, good) on turf debut; takes a step up in grade but warrants respect with further progress possible.
Very productive in the first half of 2025 but has subsequently been unable to add to his tally; admittedly ties in with a couple of today's opponents on C&D form three weeks ago (penultimate start).
Has done her winning over 5f on AW; behind a few of today's rivals over C&D most recently, taking turf record to 0-12; enough reasons to oppose her.

RaceName: Winning Experience With Moulton Racing Handicap
Spotlight: Yarmouth specialist who turned things around with a comfortable success over C&D (good to firm) last week; only 3lb higher under a penalty and he's a major player again in his bid for a fifth course win.

RaceName: Winning Experience With Moulton Racing Handicap
Spotlight: Dual turf winner who scored on AW at Chelmsford (7f) in January but he's been well held back in this sphere in last two starts; now 2lb lower than for his last success but he needs to get back on track; cheekpieces reapplied.

RaceName: Winning Experience With Moulton Racing Handicap
Spotlight: Just 1-13 on turf but that win came with a strong finish over 7f here (good) last month; held in fifth of 12 at Doncaster last time but that was a respectable effort and this step back up to 1m is no problem; in the mix.

RaceName: Winning Experience With Moulton Racing Handicap
Spotlight: Won a Southwell handicap (1m, AW) last September and he's held his form with four creditable efforts since; still looks on a workable mark and has possibilities if this sets up for his closing style.

RaceName: Winning Experience With Moulton Racing Handicap
Spotlight: Justified support in a Yarmouth maiden (6f, good to firm) last July; hasn't gone on from that in five subsequent runs but he was beaten less than 2l when fifth at Doncaster (7f; blinkers added) last time; that was eye-catching from off the pace and he's not ruled out back up in trip.

RaceName: Winning Experience With Moulton Racing Handicap
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: Made the frame in all three 2yo runs, but he started his handicap career with a remote last of six at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) this month; that was after 255 days off but he needs to bounce back with a personal best on this drop back in trip.

RaceName: Edward Foster & Son Plumbers Merchant Bradford Handicap
Spotlight: Belied her 20-1 odds with recent C&D success on good ground, taking Doncaster record to 2-5; incurs a penalty (future mark 1lb lower) but may well remain competitive kept to this venue.

RaceName: Edward Foster & Son Plumbers Merchant Bradford Handicap
Spotlight: Has done all his winning on Newcastle AW; however, finished a good second over C&D most recently and is due to go back up 1lb in future; holds a fighting chance in his attempt to go one better.

RaceName: Edward Foster & Son Plumbers Merchant Bradford Handicap
Spotlight: Productive spell in June/July 2024 featured a C&D success off 6lb higher; poor striike-rate since but he ran encouragingly at Ayr (first turf attempt of 2026) most recently; in the mix.

RaceName: Edward Foster & Son Plumbers Merchant Bradford Handicap
Spotlight: Has performed well over C&D (good to firm) in last two starts, finishing third then opening her account; this low-mileage 4yo may well improve further; respected kept to same scenario.

RaceName: Edward Foster & Son Plumbers Merchant Bradford Handicap
Spotlight: Broadly inconsistent but his form at Doncaster features a success in last year's Leger Legends race and resurgent effort behind Bella Bisbee in latest start; better off with that rival and due to go back up 1lb in future.

RaceName: Edward Foster & Son Plumbers Merchant Bradford Handicap
Spotlight: Has largely consistent form, close fourth at Leicester (7f) most recently; however, he's the only maiden in this field and chance of winning partly depends on the effects of added tongue-tie.

RaceName: Edward Foster & Son Plumbers Merchant Bradford Handicap
Spotlight: Has gained last three wins on AW, including latest start; effective on turf but has never won away from Leicester in this sphere; something to prove back up in grade.
Sole win was in a maiden in August 2024 and he's struggled in two runs (7f/5.7f) for new yard this season; has lots to prove at this new trip and this looks tough on the figures.
All six wins have come at around this trip on AW; pulled up back over hurdles last month and withdrawn in that code subsequently having been unruly at the post; best watched.

Pulled up over hurdles on his reappearance last month but he's a dual Flat winner and his last success was in this race (good; 5-2 favourite) last year; yard is in good form and he's an interesting contender back in this sphere; cheekpieces reapplied.
Tailed off at massive prices in three novice/maiden events at up to 1m2f; now drops into a classified event but she needs a transformation with cheekpieces added.
Struggled in three qualifying runs and same story when tailed off on handicap debut here (1m, good to firm) in April; still early days but she needs a visor to make a big difference at this new trip.
Beaten 12l or more in all four runs including on handicap debut at Lingfield (1m, AW) three weeks ago; now steps up to 1m2f and she needs plenty of improvement; stablemate of Raise The Stakes.

0-6 but he found improvement upped to 1m2f when runner-up in a classified event at Lingfield (good; first-time visor) last week; that was behind a rival who was completing a four-timer and he's a big player if he can build on that.

Six-race maiden who has bits of encouraging form; good second, despite traffic issues, at Beverley (8.5f) on penultimate start and was possibly unsuited by Ripon latest; first win is not inconceivable.

Ex-Irish maiden who still has low mileage; did well to finish 1l third at Kempton (1m3f, AW; came from difficult position) on stable debut and sole start this year; interesting off same mark on return from another break.

Close second at Pontefract (1m2f) on handicap debut; hasn't made the expected progress in two starts since but ran creditably with cheekpieces applied at same venue latest; possibilities if added tongue-tie works a treat.
Low-mileage 4yo has been kept to 1m since handicapping, running well on first occasion but disappointing in two starts this term; chance of a resurgence largely depends on the effects of new trip.

Has gone close on AW a couple of times for new yard but failed to beat a rival last time; 3-7 in turf handicaps and looks quite interesting back in this sphere, off 1lb below last winning mark; tongue-tie fitted.
Ex-Irish maiden who has been essentially regressive for current stable; ended up tailed off (bled from nose) last time; perhaps best watched unless the market suggests a turnaround is likely.
Has never won above Class 6 and doesn't look as if he's about to put that right; consistent spell has been terminated by his form taking a dip in last few runs.

RaceName: QuinnBet Best Odds Guaranteed "Confined" Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won In 2026)
Spotlight: Well handicapped on his best form last season but last win was almost two years and he's finished down the field in his last four runs; now drops back in grade but this is competitive and others are more persuasive.

RaceName: QuinnBet Best Odds Guaranteed "Confined" Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won In 2026)
Spotlight: Sole win was on his debut in December 2024 but he's been knocking on door for Jack Jones this spring including when runner-up over C&D (good) last month; remains well treated on best form and he's a big player on this first run for James Owen; hood removed.

RaceName: QuinnBet Best Odds Guaranteed "Confined" Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won In 2026)
Spotlight: Three wins in the first half of 2025 and latest was off 1lb higher over C&D; returned after wind surgery with a promising effort in a Class 4 here (7f, good to firm) last month and he looks interesting on this drop back in grade.

RaceName: QuinnBet Best Odds Guaranteed "Confined" Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won In 2026)
Spotlight: Won a Southwell maiden (1m, AW) last September but has mixed record in handicaps since and he was beaten 12l at Kempton (6f) last time; continues to drop down the weights but others are more convincing.

RaceName: QuinnBet Best Odds Guaranteed "Confined" Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won In 2026)
Spotlight: Record of 2-4 on AW but he's not made an impact back on turf in his last three starts; on same mark as when third of six at Lingfield (7.6f, good to firm) last time and he has some work to do.

RaceName: QuinnBet Best Odds Guaranteed "Confined" Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won In 2026)
Spotlight: Kempton novice win (6f, AW) for Oliver Cole last October; placed in two of his three runs for new yard this season but he was beaten almost 5l when third at Kempton last time; edging down the weights but he needs to find more back up in trip.

RaceName: QuinnBet Best Odds Guaranteed "Confined" Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won In 2026)
Spotlight: Unexposed 3yo who made a promising start in handicaps when a close second at Sandown (7f, good) two weeks ago; looks interesting off only 1lb higher here and he's open to more progress; key player.

RaceName: QuinnBet Best Odds Guaranteed "Confined" Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won In 2026)
Spotlight: Inconsistent 11-race maiden who produced one of his lesser efforts at Windsor (6f, good to firm) last time; this return to 7f is no problem but he needs to bounce back with cheekpieces added.

Steadily back to form this term, creditable third over C&D most recently; has slipped back to the mark off which his 2yo success was gained; this is a drop in class; therefore interesting on a few counts.

OwnerName: Come On Over Syndicate & A H Yates Ltd
Spotlight: Still a maiden but proved resurgent with rallying fourth of 14 dropped to Class 6 over C&D most recently; possibilities off same mark in same grade, provided he builds on that effort.

OwnerName: Matt Broughton & Hold My Beer Syndicate
Spotlight: Has reasonably consistent maiden/novice form; holds weaker claims on her two handicap efforts but this drop in grade may have positive effect; soft ground was a possible excuse last time.
Well beaten on debut but has shown some ability with a couple of fourth-place finishes since; could go well on handicap debut, with Lewis Chalkley taking off a handy 7lb.
Ran quite encouragingly at Leicester in his only turf attempt since handicapping; interesting back in this sphere, assuming he responds well to first-time tongue-tie and cheekpieces.
Inconsistent over 7f since switched to handicaps; prevailed at Redcar on penultimate start but isn't certain to repeat that form, especially as the return to 6f presents an added question mark.
Not particularly solid on his Class 5 form for current yard but this drop back in grade may have positive effect; recorded two of his three juvenile wins at this level.

Failed to get home over 7f on soft ground when making handicap debut last time; has shown promise otherwise and remains open to progress, being related to several winners (including one at Royal Ascot last week) for her connections; respected back down in trip.
Maiden who has inconsistent form overall and whose bare figures this season are meagre; receiving plenty of weight all round reflects the way he's been heading.

RaceName: QuinnBet Daily Free Bet Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Added to her 1m win here (good to firm) last summer when scoring over 7f at Lingfield (AW) last September; only 2lb higher than for that win and she returned after six months off with a close second at Kempton (7f) last month; now drops to 6f for the first time but has claims if she gets a good pace.

RaceName: QuinnBet Daily Free Bet Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Acts on any ground and she's a triple sprint winner on turf including over C&D; wasn't at her best at Goodwood three weeks ago but she made a bold bid at Lingfield on her penultimate run and has claims if she can recapture that form.

RaceName: QuinnBet Daily Free Bet Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins (6f/7f) have been on AW but she was disappointing when favourite at Southwell (5f, Tapeta) last week; still unexposed on turf but she has form figures of 600 in this sphere and all of those runs were in handicaps; others preferred.

RaceName: QuinnBet Daily Free Bet Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Made it fourth-time lucky when landing a Redcar novice (6f, good to firm) in April but she flopped when favourite on handicap debut at Pontefract (seventh of eighth) three weeks ago; still has potential for top yard but she needs to bounce back after that last run.

RaceName: QuinnBet Daily Free Bet Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Showed promise in maiden/novice events and she made a solid start to her handicap career when third at Leicester (6f, good to firm) last month; nudged up 1lb but she gave it a good shot from the front last time and that form was boosted when the winner followed up under a penalty; interesting contender.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap
Spotlight: Prescott runner who has shown some improvement with a pair of second-place finishes since wearing headgear, latest at Sandown (1m6f, good); turn looks near.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap
Spotlight: Flopped when favourite on handicap/seasonal debut but rebounded (good second over 1m4f) at Ffos Las since; possibilities off same mark upped further in trip.

RaceName: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap
OwnerName: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum
Spotlight: Has shown positive signs in two starts since handicapping and switched to turf, finishing second both times, latest at Ayr (1m7f) a week ago; respected off same mark.

OwnerName: Lady Emma Oshea And A Carson
Spotlight: Nine-time AW winner who is 0-23 on turf, but he's versatile ground-wise and has finished runner-up in two of his five starts at Yarmouth; hasn't threatened back on turf in last two runs but he ran respectably over C&D last time and he's in the mix back in a classified event.

9yo who has won 16 times on AW; his sole turf win came at Yarmouth but he failed to beat a rival over C&D (good) when last seen in April and needs to bounce back after that 16l defeat; cheekpieces reapplied.

Has claims on a couple of runner-up efforts over 6f (good to firm) last summer but he's still a maiden after 31 starts and was well held in classified events in his final two runs last winter; now returns to sprinting but he needs to revive after 165 days off.

Unexposed 3yo but she's not finished closer than 8l to a winner and was last of 14 in a handicap here (7f, soft) two weeks ago; has plenty to prove and she needs to show a lot more on this drop to 6f.

Beat only one rival in three maiden/novice events (7f/6f, good) but it's still early days for this well-bred filly and she's a possible improver now dropped into a 0-50 event; needs a close look in the market.

Struggled in three qualifying runs last year and same story when tailed-off on handicap debut at Ripon (1m, good) last month; may have needed that run but she needs a transformation on this return to 6f.

Won a very competitive staying handicap in Abu Dhabi at the end of last year and wasn't disgraced in two Group One races there this year; made a winning start here at Wolverhampton where he had a fitness edge and he beat two of today's rivals; track and trip should suit and despite giving weight all round he's the one to beat. (R28 W5 P7) (OR 104)

A real improver for this yard only once finishing outside of the first three in fifteen starts and that came in Group One company; tas finished behind RB Yas Sir and Al Sharid (twice) this season but is better off with both at the weights this time around; can't rule out. (R18 W6 P8) (OR 92)

Was flattered to get so close to RB Yas Sir on his debut at Wolverhampton; put that experience to good use when winning at Bath on his handicap debut under this rider last month; he was value for more than the winning margin that day and he's open to lots of improvement stepping up in trip; strong claims. (R2 W1 P1) (OR 90)

Defied an absence of five years to make a winning handicap debut at Chester two years ago and managed to win over this distance at Windsor last year; this is tougher, but he gets lots of weight all round; can't be discounted. (R5 W3 P1) (OR 82)

RaceName: RF Miller & Co Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Beaten 25l and 9l when runner-up in his first two maiden hurdles before readily justifying favouritism under Sean Bowen over C&D (good) a month ago; penalised accordingly but looks very capable of following up.

RaceName: RF Miller & Co Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Runner-up in a point and his fourth of 18 in a 2m maiden hurdle at Navan (soft) in March 2025 was a promising effort; however, confined to only two runs since and has struggled; sights are lowered here on first run since September; wouldn't be too dismissive.

RaceName: RF Miller & Co Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Two 1m wins at Dundalk for his previous yard; only seventh of 12 in a 2m6f maiden at Downpatrick but the RPR he recorded that day firmly puts him in the mix.

RaceName: RF Miller & Co Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Bought for £6,000 after finishing 21l off the winner in a 3m good-ground Irish point last month; she's an unlikely winner judged on that performance.

RaceName: RF Miller & Co Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Insufficient promise in points to make any great appeal, for all that she's a 4yo filly with the allowances and with a 10lb claimer up top to boot.
Dual novice winner for previous yard; building up some momentum for this one after beating all bar an improver at Bangor (2m7f, good); considered.
Won this two years ago after a 157-day absence when 3lb lower and has won two more since; she wasn't in great form when last seen but could easily stage a revival.
Has the form to get in contention but she had a shocker when tailed off in a first-time visor at Fakenham; regularly worn cheekpieces but now goes without any headgear.
Ten-race maiden who threatened on occasions last year, going close over 3m at Perth and Galway; tailed off in May after 199 days off but could be a different proposition this time.

Won a 2m2f handicap at Kilbeggan in April 2025 (good to yielding) and didn't run shockingly after a year out at Tramore two months ago; cheekpieces are turned to and this Irish challenger could go well.
2-6 for Adam Nichol and was only just denied off 3lb lower at Market Rasen a month ago (2m4f, good); in form and should stay this far.
Dual AW winner; 0-6 over hurdles; in first-time cheekpieces when runner-up over 2m4f at Ffos Las (good to soft; fine on good) and she wasn't stopping; interesting now sent further.

Having her 32nd career start when finally off the mark in a selling handicap over 2m1f here recently (good); only 4lb higher and new trainer James Moffatt has booked Brian Hughes; has to be of interest despite blatant stamina reservations.
Runner-up over 2m7f at Fakenham last month, since when she's finished 12l behind at Stratford; only 1-33 overall and others preferred for win purposes.

RaceName: Molson Coors Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Comfortable winner of a Kelso hunter chase (2m7f, good) last month but was beaten fair and square by about 10l in a 3m handicap at Perth since and has only dropped 1lb.

RaceName: Molson Coors Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: The tongue-tie has made a difference and he completed a Hexham four-timer five days ago (2m4f, good); only two others got round but he's on a roll and might be well in with a penalty.

RaceName: Molson Coors Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Fell and tailed off in his two previous chases in late 2023; lightly raced in the meantime but returns to this discipline after three solid runs over hurdles, all handicaps from 2m1f to 2m4f; likely to go well again if he still fancies fences.

RaceName: Molson Coors Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Three bumper wins included Listed honours and he won a maiden hurdle last summer; goes chasing on the back of a close fourth at Ffos Las (2m4f, good to soft; perhaps better on good ground) and should have a role to play if finding some fluency.

RaceName: Molson Coors Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Capable on his day for Jamie Snowden, winning three times; didn't sign off for that yard in bad form but he made a quiet debut for this yard here when 3m1f was no excuse.

RaceName: Lakes Luxury Loo's Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Won twice over hurdles at Cartmel in 2025; following a wind operation, he's finished runner-up in 3m1f/2m5f handicap chases here, last time only reeled in late on; clear claims off an untouched mark.

RaceName: Lakes Luxury Loo's Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Completed a hat-trick at around 3m during the opening quarter of 2025 but following a year out he refused to race on consecutive appearances this spring.

RaceName: Lakes Luxury Loo's Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has looked in good nick since returning from wind surgery; however, he was close up behind Caughtinyourtrance here last month and he's 8lb higher now after winning a Class 3 handicap over 3m5f here two days later by 7.5l (Fairlawn Flyer third); he's still a major player with James Murray, aboard last time, again up top.

RaceName: Lakes Luxury Loo's Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: All good since joining Stuart Edmonds and deserved his 2m7f win over hurdles at Uttoxeter (by 4l; good); up 4lb for this return to fences but he's a three-time winner in this discipline.

RaceName: Lakes Luxury Loo's Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Admirable strike-rate of 10-29 over fences; won this 12 months ago off 1lb higher and had only five starts in the meantime, the latest respectable in a higher grade behind stablemate Stratagem; chance.

RaceName: Lakes Luxury Loo's Handicap Chase (GBB Race)
Spotlight: None too consistent but he was back over fences at Punchestown 20 days ago (3m1f, good to yielding) and ran on well to prevail in a big field; that was his fourth success as a chaser and big shout if he can hold his form.
Won three on the bounce over hurdles in February (2m-2m4f, good to soft/soft) but has lost his form in the blink of an eye and finished last on his recent return to chasing (0-8).

First win for a long time and over fences when it was never in much doubt here a month ago (2m1f, good; first-time cheekpieces); went up 8lb but Charlotte Jones announced her retirement straight after that race so 10lb claimer Leah Noreci now takes over; weighted to go well again and this longer trip won't trouble him.
Ex-Irish ten-race maiden who is also 0-4 in points; tailed off in his two completed starts for this yard, finishing well behind Out On Her Own in one of those.
Three-time course winner but he's had his off days here as well, including a C&D effort last month; he's still hard to dismiss off a perfectly good mark.

Ran some fair races in defeat over hurdles and a similar story in two runs over fences in races won by the improving Passengerontheship who runs earlier on this card; hood fitted for the penultimate run returns; might not be far away.

0-5 over hurdles; won an Irish point so no surprise to see him run well for a long way on chase debut at Newton Abbot (2m5f, good); he was beaten about 17l in the end and two new forms of headgear are now enlisted, but couldn't rule out.
Longstanding maiden who often races freely and he's 3lb out of the weights for this second run over fences; he was well held in the first.

Wootton Bassett colt; E2,300,000 yearling; half-brother to winners Highbury (Group-placed 1m4f; RPR 111), Friendly Face (French/Swiss Listed-placed 1m1f-10.5f; 100) and Mille Fois Merci (French 1m4f; 78); dam French 5.5f (Group 3) and 7f 2yo winner (111); one of three for top yard, Group 1 entered and the choice of Moore.

Dark Angel colt; E120,000 foal, E280,000 yearling; closely related to 5.7f-7f winner Beauty Crescent (inc 2yo Listed/HK; RPR 105), half-brother to four winners inc Worship (6f AW 2yo; 100); dam 6f-1m winner (inc 2yo/US 1m Grade 2; 108); likely type on paper for yard that has won four of the last five renewals.
Promise on debut at Cork (6f, soft) but didn't build on that when soundly beaten at Naas dropped to 5f on quicker ground; others make more appeal now.

Justify colt; 275,000USD yearling; half-brother to US 7.5f turf 2yo winner Orbit; dam 7f 2yo winner (RPR 91), half-sister to winners Order Of St George (Gold Cup and Irish St Leger), Asperity (1m Group 3) and Angel Terrace (US 8.5f Grade 3); one of three newcomers for top yard; overlooked by Moore.
Starman colt is one of two for yard; ran green when a 9.25l third of 9 on debut at Navan (6f, good) when an 11-2 shot; tongue tie goes on; looks the stable's second string on rider arrangements.

OwnerName: Exors of the Late Mrs Dolores Gleeson
Spotlight: Hello Youmzain colt sent off at 7-2 on debut at Cork (5f, soft) but was beaten 8l into fifth of 10; top rider booked and steps up in trip now on faster ground but likely best watched.

OwnerName: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg
Spotlight: No Nay Never colt; brother to 6f/7f winner Hamlet (inc AW; RPR 86), closely related to 7f AW 2yo winner Velazquez (85), half-brother to 1m-1m4f winner Perfect Your Craft (inc 2yo; 97); dam Listed-placed 1m2f winner (103); overlooked by Moore but Group 1 entered and not discounted.

Starman colt; E50,000 yearling; half-brother to winners See All Match (Qatari 8.5f/1m2f) and Petarda (Italian/Slovakian 8.5f/1m1f Flat inc Listed; 2m-2m3f hurdle); dam unplaced 7f-1m4f (RPR 65); probably best watched unless market speaks.

Starman colt; E12,000 yearling; half-brother to Listed-placed 6f 2yo winner Figjam (RPR 91); dam 1m/8.6f AW winner (79), half-sister to useful 6f-1m5f winner Karam Albaari; displayed promise when third in Naas barrier trial.
Bayside Boy colt made rapid late gains for a 1l third of 17 in a Fairyhouse maiden on debut two weeks ago; should be more streetwise with that under his belt and sets the standard on form.

Earthlight colt; E32,000 yearling; half-brother to winners Letsbefrankaboutit (6f 2yo Group 3; RPR 103), Lady Ayresome (5f 2yo; 86), Moojim (5f-6f inc 2yo/Sweden; 86) and Miyagi (KSA 7f/10.5f); dam 6f 2yo winner (70); showed ability when fifth of 10 in Naas barrier trial; not ruled out.
Huge odds and well beaten in all three previous starts; seems safe to rule out.

RaceName: Zyn In The Moment British EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Second foal; half-sister to 6f 2yo winner Crimson Rose (RPR 87); dam French 5f/7f winner (incuding 2yo/Group 3; 108), half-sister to useful 7f 2yo winner Pacifica Pier, out of 7f 2yo Listed winning sister to US 7f Grade 2 winner True Quality; interesting newcomer from top stable.

RaceName: Zyn In The Moment British EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 160,000gns yearling; third foal; half-sister to 5f AW 2yo winner Mwaki (RPR 62); unraced dam closely related to 5f Group 2 winner Priceless, half-sister to smart 6f-1m winner Doctor Brown; could be against two useful fillies on her debut but still makes some appeal.

RaceName: Zyn In The Moment British EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 230,000gns yearling; first foal; dam 5f 2yo winner (including Listed; RPR 101), closely related to 1m4f winner Sioux Princess, out of French 8.5f 2yo winning half-sister to Listed winners Power Elite (7f 2yo) and La Sylvia (6f); looks the part on paper.

RaceName: Cartmel Priory Cofe School Amateur Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 10lb higher than for his Perth win last June (2m, good), after which he ran well on the Flat at Galway; back from 289 days off when beaten about 12l at Wexford and that could have been needed; on a tough mark, though.

RaceName: Cartmel Priory Cofe School Amateur Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Useful hunter chase form was a long time ago, and he was unable to win any of his last six points; very opposable returned to hurdles, especially at this trip.

RaceName: Cartmel Priory Cofe School Amateur Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Middleham Park Cxv, Lycett Rcg & Partner
Spotlight: Has been running on the Flat, winning 1m4f/1m2f handicaps before a respectable effort in a competitive race at York; capable hurdler with winning form here and James Murray takes off a useful 7lb.

RaceName: Cartmel Priory Cofe School Amateur Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Only 1-11 over hurdles and the win came in a maiden; however, this mark shouldn't be beyond her and she was quite free after a break when beaten 13l into third at Sedgefield (2m1f, good); has form here.

RaceName: Cartmel Priory Cofe School Amateur Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: The North West National Hunt Racing Club
Spotlight: Won twice on the Flat and over hurdles; had been chasing before beaten 9l in a Bangor handicap hurdle (2m, good) and that was after 173 days away; on last winning mark if he can find something on that form.

RaceName: Cartmel Priory Cofe School Amateur Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Second run back from an absence when scoring comfortably at Plumpton (2m, good) to earn this 8lb higher mark; hasn't had a lot of racing and he's Chris Gordon's only runner on today's card.

RaceName: Cartmel Priory Cofe School Amateur Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Not beaten all that far in 2m6f/3m1f handicaps on last two starts but he was a weakener late on; others appeal more.

RaceName: Cartmel Priory Cofe School Amateur Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: He has competitive form here but not last month when tailed off in a selling handicap; that was after 167 days away but he had to show more.

RaceName: Cartmel Priory Cofe School Amateur Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Didn't last long with the Skeltons, though he was second in a bumper and in a handicap hurdle; last seen winning a match between the flags in May 2025; needs checking in the betting on first run for Neil King.

RaceName: Diageo Luxury Spirits Irish EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: Made a promising debut at Leopardstown last autumn and built on that on return when a close second in a Gowran maiden over this trip; a leading player with improvement to come.

RaceName: Diageo Luxury Spirits Irish EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: Poor in two maidens in 2024 and not a lot better on return at Limerick a week ago; wait for handicaps.

RaceName: Diageo Luxury Spirits Irish EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: Sixth foal; half-brother to French winners Metropolitan (1m inc 2yo/French 2,000 Guineas; RPR 118) and Mister Charlie (7.5f/1m inc AW; 81); dam unraced sister to 1m3f winner Dorfman; cost E150,000 as a yearling; makes appeal on paper so needs a check in the market for a yard flying high.

RaceName: Diageo Luxury Spirits Irish EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: Well beaten in two maidens and will be of more interest in handicaps after this.

RaceName: Diageo Luxury Spirits Irish EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: Has run some solid races in defeat up to 1m2f; faced a tough task at Gowran last time but ran creditably; the hood worn then is left off and the drop back to 1m should be fine so is a player.

RaceName: Diageo Luxury Spirits Irish EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: Second foal; dam 9.4f/1m2f winner (inc 2yo/AW; RPR 87), half-sister to 1m Listed winner Staying On, from the family of Oaks winner Talent; 3,000gns foal was a 1,000gns vendor buyback as a yearling; looks the stable second-string on debut.

RaceName: Diageo Luxury Spirits Irish EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: Third foal; dam unplaced 1m 2yo (RPR 61), half-sister to Listed-placed 9.5f 2yo winner Delhi, out of Group-placed 6f 2yo winner; not a bad pedigree and cost E10,000 as a yearling; yard sprang a 25/1 shock in this race last year so not ruled out.

RaceName: Diageo Luxury Spirits Irish EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: Was beaten 20l on debut over 1m2f at Fairyhouse so would have to make huge progress in three weeks.

RaceName: Diageo Luxury Spirits Irish EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: Closely related to 7f 2yo winner Royal Meeting (inc Group 1; RPR 116), half-brother to four winners inc Heavy Metal (5f-1m inc 2yo turf Group 2/UAE dirt Group 2; 119); dam SA 6f Grade 1 winner/UAE 1,000 Guineas third (95); 30,000gns yearling has a quality international damline; looks yard's first-string so needs a market check.

RaceName: Diageo Luxury Spirits Irish EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: Was strong in the market on debut when just denied over 7f at Leopardstown; the form of that race has taken some knocks but he should have improved since and the step up in trip should be fine.

RaceName: Diageo Luxury Spirits Irish EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: Solid in two runs as a 2yo; unlucky-in-running on return at Leopardstown but ran a fine race there last time over 1m2f; caught close home then after leading in the straight so the drop to 1m should suit; a player.

RaceName: Diageo Luxury Spirits Irish EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: Beaten 17l on debut over 7f at Leopardstown recently and will have to step up plenty on that.

RaceName: Diageo Luxury Spirits Irish EBF Median Auction Maiden
Spotlight: Eighth foal; half-sister to five winners inc Anda Muchacho (Italian 1m-1m2f inc Group 2; RPR 112), Parsley (5f/6f 2yo; 99) and Love Powerful (6f inc 2yo; 91); decent pedigree and cost 220,000gns as a yearling; worth a market check for an in-form yard.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Newcastle Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: 0-8 but he's finished runner-up twice and wasn't far away on last two outings either (upped to 1m4f latest); tongue tied on last two, with cheekpieces as well on latest; acts on Tapeta; probably thereabouts.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Newcastle Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: No win since June 2024 off 15lb higher and he's never made much of an impact in his five AW races; he's still shown more than enough on turf this season to be considered, however.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Newcastle Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Second five times at up to 1m2f, including in first blinkers at Beverley in April on yard debut; heavy defeat over 1m4f four weeks ago sees a visor return today; 0-21 tally (0-5 on AW) is probably the bottom line.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Newcastle Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins last season were over 1m2f, latterly over C&D in September off 1lb lower than today; two minor honours from three starts this turf season ensure that he has a prominent place in calculations.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Newcastle Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Cheekpieces seemed to have a positive effect early in the year, notably for a 1m2f win on Chelmsford Polytrack, but he was soundly beaten in them on penultimate start and below form when hooded on latest; now he has cheekpieces plus a first-time tongue-tie; lightly raced but none too solid.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Newcastle Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
OwnerName: R Whitelock, L Clemitson & R Jefferson
Spotlight: Had a hurdles win in spring 2025 and 2026; 0-9 on Flat but two C&D seconds early this year put him in the picture.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Newcastle Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: 1m2f classified event here in March was his only win from 12 races; returned to some form when third at Beverley (8.5f) on latest start; return to 1m2f should suit if this is another of his good days.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Newcastle Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
OwnerName: Brian Ellison Racing Club & M Dale
Spotlight: Not seen since runner-up over fences in December 2024 and his light AW record is less convincing than that on turf.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Newcastle Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Closest finish when beaten 6l on latest start but still managed only ninth of 11 in that 9.4f handicap at Wolverhampon (Tapeta; just 7-1) when tongue tied first time, after 231 days off; lightly raced but lacks solid claims.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Newcastle Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Back-to-back wins last summer in six-runner turf handicaps over 1m2f/1m4f; back to form on last two outings, which suggests an each-way chance despite the patchy record in his five AW outings.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Newcastle Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Latest of his two wins was in 2021; runner-up over hurdles last May but very opposable on what he's done since; off 260 days before latest start when he was remote at 200-1; first-time headgear this evening.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Newcastle Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Inconsistent for three previous yards, winning just once (last August) since 2023; satisfactory debut for new trainer when third at Chepstow (1m2f) 11 days ago, however, and probably effective here, so needs a second look.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Newcastle Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: 28-race maiden; second on Southwell Tapeta (1m6f) last October and bits of form since, but others have to be preferred.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Newcastle Amateur Jockeys' Handicap
Spotlight: Triple-figure prices for all four starts, the latest her handicap debut at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW; made most) when finishing a lot closer but in only eighth of 11; no form claims.

RaceName: John Banks Group Front Runner Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Well beaten at triple-digit odds on both starts, the latter her seasonal/stable debut at Windsor (1m, good to soft) this month.

RaceName: John Banks Group Front Runner Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: First foal; dam 1m 2yo winner (RPR 90), half-sister to St Leger winner Logician; encountered some traffic before running on well to finish second at Yarmouth (7f, good to soft) 16 days ago; ought to be hard to beat.

RaceName: John Banks Group Front Runner Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 650,000euros yearling; half-sister to a French Listed winner; dam half-sister to French 1m4f Group 1 winner Left Hand; showed her inexperience and finished over 3l behind Starlight Lass when seventh of 11 at Yarmouth (7f, good to soft) this month but has obvious scope for improvement.
Dual winner over 1m1f but has placed form at this trip too; way below her best in two starts this year but this faster ground will suit and can give an improved effort.
Five wins between turf and AW for Johnny Murtagh; best effort for this yard came when fourth over 7f here but has been below that level twice again since; blinkers added.
Won three times last year up to a mile; hasn't been near that level this year but last time at Down Royal was a bit better; needs a return to best.
Has been consistent in defeat in six maidens up to 1m2.5f; carried her head awkwardly at Dundalk last time and a visor is applied; needs her best on handicap bow.

OwnerName: O B P Carroll & Anthony Vaughan
Spotlight: Won a Leopardstown maiden over this trip last year; was below her best on final two starts of 2025; has to improve on her C&D comeback run last time.
All six wins have been gained on AW when trained in UK; some fine runs in defeat for this yard on AW over the winter; soft ground was against him the last twice and can do better.
Two wins over this trip when trained in UK; soundly beaten in four runs over various trips for this yard last year; needs his best on return but the lively ground will suit.
Got off the mark for former yard in a 6f Navan maiden last October; has placed form at this trip but needs to do better than on his two starts for this yard.
Won handicaps at Gowran and over C&D last year, both with plenty of cut in the ground; some fine efforts in defeat this term including on good ground; needs a bit more but considered.
Won a Dundalk maiden over 7f on second start last winter; a fine effort on handicap/seasonal debut over 7f here but wasn't as good over C&D since when raced keenly; questions now.
Was runner-up in a Ripon maiden over 1m2f last August on second start for former yard; well below that level in two runs for this yard but faster ground back to 1m may suit.
Got off the mark in a Gowran handicap over this trip and was a close second at Ballinrobe (1m1.5f) under a 7lb penalty after; was poor at Leopardstown since so questions now.
Dual AW winner gained her sole turf win from 27 starts over 7f at this venue last year; soundly beaten on last three starts and needs a return to best.
All three wins have been here including one over C&D; was third on return over C&D but was poor at Leopardstown since so has questions to answer now.
Sole win came in a 7f Brighton maiden in 2024; some fair runs for this yard since last year but has to improve on recent form.
Won a valuable 1m2f Goodwood novice for former yard in 2025 but lost her form latterly; the best of four runs for this yard came over C&D last time and can do better so considered.

OwnerName: J Kirkland & Mrs J M Ryan
Spotlight: Made a promising debut over 7f here last year behind the brilliant Diamond Necklace; didn't seem to handle soft ground next time at Cork; well beaten by a smart rival in a Dundalk maiden on return; considered on handicap debut and the market could help.
Sole win came over C&D last year off 4lb lower; several decent efforts in defeat since including at Limerick last time though looked a bit awkward under pressure then; still, unlikely to be far away.
Dual AW winner has only had three starts on turf but didn't run badly over C&D last time; still needs a fair bit more dropped 2lb.

Won a 7f Dundalk maiden on third start last December; was poor at the same track next time; came back after a break with a fine effort over C&D when a bit keen; needs a bit more but a chance off 2lb lower.
Consistent in defeat in three maidens last year; beaten 12l on return in a 1m2f Down Royal handicap; visor returns down in trip and needs his best.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Shiremoor Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 37,000gns foal, 20,000gns yearling; second foal; brother to 7f AW/1m2f turf winner Titainium (RPR 77); dam unraced half-sister to two classy winners; refused to enter stalls and withdrawn from this month's intended debut at Thirsk (40-1 at the time); probably best watched.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Shiremoor Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Peter Onslow, FDC Holdings Ltd & NWIR Ltd
Spotlight: 28-1 when soundly beaten third of five at 28-1 on debut in a warm novice at York (1m, good to firm) last May; he's been absent since but that was an encouraging start to his career and he's not ruled out on his return.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Shiremoor Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Always towards the rear on last month's debut at Kempton (7f, AW; 10-1) and beaten 8l when sixth of 13 at 66-1 at Salisbury (7f, soft) last time; plenty of improvement is needed but this 160,000euros yearling is from a respected Newmarket stable and could take a big step forward at some point.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Shiremoor Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Left last month's debut run behind when fourth of 11 at Doncaster (7f, good to firm); his illustrious pedigree provides optimism that he will continue to progress and he holds leading claims.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Shiremoor Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Well beaten at 25-1 then 100-1 this month on his first two starts (1m2f/7f, good to firm) and a transformation is needed.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Shiremoor Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Closely related to 7f 2yo winner Courageous Knight (RPR 94) but beaten a long way at triple-figure odds on his first two starts.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Shiremoor Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Fifth foal; Masar half-brother to 1m2f winner Tiberio Force (RPR 60); dam 1m2f/1m4f winner (95) from family of Irish Oaks winner Bolas; it seems likely this newcomer will come into his own over further.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Shiremoor Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 33-1 and slowly away on last month's debut at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW), but his 5l third of six was an encouraging effort and the runner-up won next time; could be sharper for that initial experience; possible player.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Shiremoor Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Seventh foal; Lope De Vega brother to winners Duke De Sessa (1m-1m4f including Australian Group 1), Walsingham (1m; RPR 95) and Newtown Duke (1m AW; 84), half-brother to two winners; makes debut with yard in fine form and he's one to consider, especially if strong in the betting.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Shiremoor Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Close third over C&D last month on second start and another good run when second at Pontefract (6f, good to soft) last time, making the running on each occasion; solid claims.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Shiremoor Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Not devoid of promise when keeping on for sixth of eight on last month's debut at Pontefract (6f, good to soft) but well beaten at 200-1 at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) since and others are preferred.

Front-running C&D hat-trick on good to firm last summer started with a win in this race; as good as ever in 5f events at Bath this year and a subsequent Listed defeat on good to soft at Haydock (28-1) can be glossed over; yard also won this in 2022 and she's likely to be on her game.

Often leads; C&D winner last July; two narrow defeats from three starts since cheekpieces have been fitted, over 7f at Yarmouth and 6f at York (both on good); a very likely contender back over this C&D.

Won 6f maiden and novice events (good to firm) as 2yo and had a difficult weights task when beaten under 6l in C&D Listed race on the Rowley course in October; two good efforts in 7f handicaps, on turf and AW, this year and likely to get the good pace needed now back at 6f; thereabouts.
Multiple wins include one over 7f on the Rowley course and she's also finished close up over 6f there; needs more than she's been showing of late though and this is her first run on the July course.

Maiden who acquitted herself well in some tough 5f races as 2yo, particularly when third in her group in the Group 2 Queen Mary at Royal Ascot; returned with a good third of six in a fillies' conditions race on Kempton AW (1m) in March but subsequent defeats in novice events haven't approached that form; goes handicapping over 6f with a query to answer.

OwnerName: Moulton Racing And Mrs A Jordan
Spotlight: Confirmed the promise of her first two starts when dictating the pace to her advantage in a 6f maiden on Lingfield AW in April; open to improvement now handicapping back on turf but she refused to go in the stalls at the start of the month and there'll be no easy lead on a plate today.

Has been threatening to get off the mark since the autumn and things have fallen right for her of late, following a 6f win at Leicester (good to firm) with an impressive one on Lingfield AW (7f); strong traveller who shouldn't mind the return to 6f; still has potential.

Seemingly boosted by change of headgear when winning by 6l over C&D (good) in first-time blinkers in April; obvious contender if judged on that evidence but ran a lacklustre race at Aintree last month and it's hard to tell whether he'll be in the right mood in first-time visor today.

Good-ground specialist with nine chase wins on his CV; long in the tooth now but returned from break with two respectable runs this spring and has edged his way back down to a good mark; no surprise if he makes a bold bid from the front.

Never really got going during light campaign over hurdles last season but has some fairly useful form on his CV and made quite encouraging start to chasing career when fourth at Worcester (2m4f, good) last month; can't be ignored.

OwnerName: The North West National Hunt Racing Club
Spotlight: Ex-French 5yo who was left clear at final fence to win comfortably by 11l over C&D (good) last month, his third start in Britain; 7lb higher today but open to further progress and needs to be taken very seriously.

Smart form in France for Andre Fabre; best effort for this yard on latest, a 4l fourth of 6 in conditions race at Fairyhouse when racing keenly; rider claims a handy 5lb and could go well back in handicap company.
Eight wins in France included a Listed race in April 2024; poor form over hurdles and on the Flat since joining this yard; hard to fancy but mark sliding and worth monitoring in the market.
Won on the dirt over 7f at Jebel Ali in March 2025 for former yard; unraced on turf; off since November and not easy to assess on stable and handicap debut; market can guide.

OwnerName: Thomas J O'Connor & Ms Hazel McGuinness
Spotlight: Eight-time AW winner; not as good on turf (1-31) and last of 12 on his favoured surface when last seen at Lingfield in early April; others appeal more.

7f AW maiden winner; 0-9 on turf but has finished runner up five times, including over 6f at Naas (good) in April; same mark now and didn't enjoy the smoothest of passages in two runs here since; could go well with Keane up.

Handicap debutant; promise in all three maidens at 7f-1m on AW and on easy ground on turf; had Zenford 5l behind when runner up over C&D in March and he has since advertised that form; respected, especially if strong in the betting.

Galway maiden winner last July (7f, yielding); promising start in handicaps on easy ground this term but progress stalled on quicker ground at Cork on latest in first-time cheekpieces; may prefer softer ground.

Fairyhouse maiden winner in May 2025 (6f, good); failed to fire on handicap bow at Gowran when last seen in June 2025 (7f, soft) but ground may not have suited; worth a market check on first start since.

2-6 on turf on the Flat, both wins coming on quick ground at 7f-1m at Ayr; remains 10lb above his last turf win but not beaten far off 2lb higher on latest at Musselburgh (7f, good); conditions to suit here and he could go well.

Off the mark at the fourth attempt in maidens at this venue when last sighted in September (6f, yld-sft); has placed form at 7f on good ground so worth checking in the market on handicap debut and seasonal reappearance.

Gained second career win on latest over C&D (good) and the runner up Zenford has won since; 5lb rise for that short-head success seems fair and his C&D form figures now read 311; should be in the mix again.

Good ground 7f maiden winner for former yard; narrowly denied over 6f (good) at Navan on latest; return to 7f no problem but 2lb higher now and these are deeper waters; more needed to get head back in front.

OwnerName: Mark Feighery & Kevin Blake & Paul McKeon
Spotlight: AW winner in France (8.5f); ran well at Dundalk in March (7f) but no impact on turf the last twice, albeit was out of depth in Listed race at Gowran last time; tongue tie now tried.

Three-time winner at 6f; made all to win on testing ground here in March; solid effort on latest at Cork (7f, sft-hvy) when a 1.5l second of 8; dropped 1lb for that but would prefer softer ground than this.

OwnerName: Nigel O'Hare & Martin W White & Mark Devlin & C G Bryson
Spotlight: In fine form this year; won over C&D (good) early last month off 9lb lower; solid effort in defeat over 1m (good) off revised mark here on latest; drop back to 7f no problem and top rider booked; should be thereabouts again.

0-7 on turf but has races in her; placed on handicap debut and seasonal return at Naas off 5lb higher (7f, soft); below best twice since, however, and faster ground a concern here (unraced on quicker than yielding).

OwnerName: Mark Devlin & Mark McGuckin & Claire Wylie
Spotlight: Four-time AW winner is 1-13 on turf; pleasing effort when a 2l fifth of 14 at Down Royal (7f, good) in May when not getting the clearest of runs and same mark now; this a tougher race, though, so more needed.
Dual turf winner has shaped nicely enough in two runs since return from a two-month break; not beaten that far when fourth of 15 at Leopardstown latest (7f, good) and place claims if backing that up.

Three-time C&D winner, including this race last year, who ran well when seventh of 24 in the Kensington Palace at Royal Ascot last week; she's below her last winning mark and has strong claims back down in grade.

Went close over C&D in March on stable debut and another good run when fourth at Musselburgh (1m) next time; below par at York on his last two starts but he tackled 1m2f last time and is not ruled out back down to 1m.
Won on stable/seasonal debut at Pontefract (1m, good to soft) and he's remained in good heart on both starts since (1m, good/good to firm); each-way claims provided the return to AW isn't an issue.

OwnerName: Cottier, Racing Breaks & Old Gold Racing
Spotlight: Drew a blank during regressive 2025 campaign and down the field on reappearance at Chepstow three weeks ago; however, he left last season's comeback run well behind to post 33-1 third on his next start, and he's not discounted off a reduced mark.

He hasn't been at his very best this year and a refusal to settle is becoming an issue; however, he was returning from a break at Doncaster three weeks ago and that run may have got the freshness out of him; impressive when winning two in a row over C&D last autumn and not written off.

Won 6f maiden here last November and this lightly raced 4yo has got closer to the target with each of his three turf handicap runs, keeping on for fourth at Doncaster (7f) last time in first-time cheekpieces (retained); the return to Newcastle could be a plus and the step up to 1m is worth a go.

Down the field on three of his four starts for this yard, including at Doncaster three weeks ago on latest outing; however, prior to that he caught the eye with a close fourth at Nottingham and he's 4lb lower today; capable of going well.
Off the mark at the ninth attempt at Carlisle (7f, soft) last month; soundly beaten at Doncaster (7f, good) since but it's possible he'll be able to bounce back; has run well on Tapeta elsewhere.

He's 9lb lower than when winning at Doncaster last March and may have needed his comeback run there this March, when mid-division; however, his form went the wrong way as last year developed and he needs to get back on track.

Won Kempton maiden (1m, Polytrack) in January on fourth start and initially continued in good form in handicaps there; well beaten at Kempton (1m) then Salisbury (1m2f, soft) on last two starts; the return to AW and drop back in trip may help but she has something to prove.

OwnerName: C Booth, P Boyle, S Coleby & S Smith
Spotlight: Won 7f 2yo maiden here last June on sole previous course visit and running well in defeat in handicaps this year, most recently third at Carlisle (1m, good to firm) when making the running; he's one to consider.

3yo who won on handicap/seasonal debut at Lingfield (7f, Polytrack) in April and good second at Kempton (1m, Polytrack) next time; disappointing at Musselburgh three weeks ago but could leave that behind back on AW; well worth a second look with yard in excellent form.

Had been expensive to follow but he got off the mark at the 17th attempt at Wolverhampton (8.6f, Tapeta) this month and has since been runner-up there (9.4f); also effective here and he's an each-way player.

He's 9lb lower than when third in this last year and he returned to form when going close over C&D early this month; has a modest strike-rate but he can be bang there if backing up that latest performance.

All wins at 6f on ground ranging from good to soft to heavy, the last three in 2024; well below best on final starts for previous trainer and he's beaten a total of one rival in two AW runs (1m) for this one in 2026; has a lot to prove.

Rarely seen on turf and below best since a close call on AW before Christmas but he had swamped his rivals at Redcar (1m, good) last September and was seriously hampered on the Rowley course after; weighted to go well and respected; usual aids missing but two wins minus headgear in 2025.

No great progress in AW handicaps from last October to January but he found a powerful finish in his first turf run at this level when winning over C&D (good) last week; this could take more winning under a penalty (goes up the same amount in future) but he's open to improvement.

All races at up to 7f until finishing close up over 1m on the Rowley course in May, then held off an in-form rival at Nottingham (also on good ground); seemed to find 1m1f on soft a shade too much at Lingfield since but her claims are more solid than most now back at 1m.
Three AW wins at 1m and 7f in midwinter and he wasn't too far off his best at Wolverhampton recently but his two turf runs, most recently at Yarmouth in May, leave him with some questions hanging.

33-1 winner of 6f maiden at Yarmouth as 2yo but she's found handicaps rather hard work since and was well back when upped to 1m on the Rowley course in May on her second run for this yard; needs so much more.

0-7 but he's shown enough at 1m on the AW to think there's a race in him and perhaps the first-time hood (now discarded) allied to good to soft ground combined against him back on turf at Ripon in April; sold for 8,000gns since; worth a look on debut for new yard.

Not at best when third of four in first-time blinkers (retained) last month but started off for Nick Scholfield with two good efforts in cheekpieces (one over C&D) and was recently given a wind op; big player if quickly back on song.

Launched chasing career with two heavy defeats in the autumn (3m/2m4f) but returns to racing under rules after winning good-ground maiden point by 11l last month; interesting to see what the market makes of her.

0-10 over hurdles when based in Ireland and it was her 11th run in a maiden point there when prevailing by a neck on good ground last month; now with top stable for this chasing debut, though, having recently changed hands for £5,000.
Unplaced all seven hurdle runs, having been well beaten in two 2m3f handicaps here this spring; needs combination of new trip and switch to fences to prompt significant improvement.

Four-time chase winner; returned from break with three good efforts this spring, including a C&D third (good), but looked very laboured when well beaten at Southwell 17 days ago; needs to bounce back.
Won three-runner point in April but struggled when back in a handicap chase three weeks ago, and this veteran needs to respond very positively to a change of headgear (usually wears cheekpieces).
Returned from break with two below-par runs towards the end of last season and suffered mid-race mishap on latest outing this month but he has slipped to a good mark and can't be ruled out.

Came good with narrow win off lowly mark at Stratford (2m6f, good; first-time cheekpieces) in September but last month's reappearance run was disappointing; given another wind op (his second) since; others are more persuasive.

Placed for first time under rules when clear second to a next-time-out winner at Uttoxeter (2m5f, good) last month but was pulled up (bled from the nose) on latest outing; back up in trip with tongue-tie added here; not an easy one to predict.
Won a point last year and is still unexposed under rules but was pulled up over C&D on handicap/chase debut in April; difficult to enthuse over.

Dual-purpose; two-time Flat winner in 2024 off 2lb lower at Roscommon over 10.5f (good) and off 3lb lower 7.5f (heavy); in process of running a big race on return from break when unseating in maiden hurdle at Tramore in April; could go well.

Dual-purpose; 3-14 on the Flat; won off 3lb lower on penultimate at Navan (1m5f, gd-yld); decent effort off revised mark over 1m6f here on latest (good), staying on for a 6.75l seventh of 24; won over this distance last August so drop in trip not a concern; in the mix.

Hurdles winner is 1-11 on turf on the Flat; on long losing run but went close behind Kool One at Navan in April (1m5f, gd-yld); respectable efforts twice since but more needed to get head back in front; cheekpieces tried.

OwnerName: Martin W Walsh & Tony Carey & Brian Murray & Mark Hopkins
Spotlight: AW winner; promising effort on return from break at Leopardstown on latest (1m2f, good), rallying for a 2l third of 11; well worth a go at this trip on that evidence and 1lb lower now too.

Maiden; standout effort for former yard a 3l third of 9 in 7f Tipperary maiden (soft) in May 2024; no show in two starts last year and bought by current connections for E3,000 in November; lacks a run and can only be watched.

Dual-purpose; 2-15 on the Flat, the latest win coming over 2m1f (good) at Limerick last June off 2lb lower; pulled up in chase after break on latest but ran respectably in this last year off 6lb higher so could run into the money.

Two-time winner on easy ground, including on latest at Gowran (9.5f, yielding); won over 1m3f (yielding) at Down Royal last season; 3lb rise for latest success seems reasonable and worth a try at this trip but could probably do with some rain.

Two-time AW winner (both 1m4f) is 1-16 on turf; good effort behind Kool One on penultimate at Navan (1m5f, gd-yld); didn't get the smoothest of runs when midfield at Gowran latest; drop back to 1m4f a plus and not out of this.

Two handicap wins last season, both at Leopardstown over 1m4f (good) and 1m5f (good); remains 4lb above the latest of those wins but 2m1f may have stretched last time at Limerick and he drops back in trip now; player.
Dropped into 0-60 company and back to form when a 0.5l third of 14 at Down Royal (1m5f, soft) a week ago; needs to back that up now on quicker ground in this tougher grade.
AW winner in Britain (8.5f) is 0-4 on turf and yet to hit the frame; new yard now (bought for 5,500gns) and likely best watched on first run for 239 days unless the market speaks.

OwnerName: Mrs Richard Henry & Mrs John Magnier
Spotlight: 3yo filly; progressive since sent handicapping; notched up wins at Dundalk over 8f and 10.5f before fine effort off 2lb lower at Leopardstown on latest (1m4f, good); could be more to come with tongue tie applied.

0-7 but has a race in him based on his two handicap runs at Leopardstown, including on latest when beaten 5.5l into third of 15 (1m1f, soft, Pillar Of Hope fourth); unproven at this trip, chance if he stays (not certain on breeding).

3yo gelding is 2-2 in handicaps against his own age group; won over 1m4f at Cork (good) and followed up off 5lb higher at Leopardstown (1m4f, good); raised another 6lb and takes on older rivals now but could be more to come in first-time cheekpieces; leading claims.

3yo was narrowly denied by Darius Dark at Cork (1m4f, good) in April and is weighted to reverse that form; weakened when upped to 2m1f at Limerick (yielding) on latest; drop in trip and return to quicker ground both positives; big player.
3yo; handicap debutant; glimmers of ability in trio of maidens on varying ground at around 1m2f; worth a market check upped in trip.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Delves Hoppings Fillies' Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Made it 6-7 on AW (including a victory on Tapeta) when winning the Group 3 Winter Derby at Lingfield (1m2f, Polytrack) in February; not at her best in Group 2 at Chester since but that was a hotter race than this and she can give a good account back in a Group 3 and back on the AW; 3lb penalty.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Delves Hoppings Fillies' Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who posted a Listed third when upped in trip at Nottingham (1m2f, good) on reappearance and soft ground may not have suited in Germany next time; she has something to find in today's company but is unexposed at 1m2f and 1-1 on AW (Polytrack); not ruled out each-way.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Delves Hoppings Fillies' Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Listed winner at Saint-Cloud (1m2f, soft) last October and reappeared with respectable Listed second in France (1m2f, soft) last month; has run well here in the past, but this 5yo's form needs to go to a new level today if she's to come out on top.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Delves Hoppings Fillies' Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Won this last year prior to finishing placed in two North American Grade 1s; not at her best when third of fourth in Group 2 at York on last month's reappearance but it wasn't a bad run and she may well have needed it; very much the one to beat if reproducing anything close to her peak form.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Delves Hoppings Fillies' Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
OwnerName: Hot To Trot Racing 2 & J Fill
Spotlight: 4yo who has raced only five times, winning three of them (including on Polytrack; unraced on Tapeta); improved again to land 1m2f Listed race at Haydock and, while Diamond Rain has stronger form, this filly is certainly progressive and could yet scale loftier heights.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Delves Hoppings Fillies' Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Listed winner for Joseph O'Brien at Lingfield last June but not at the same level on final two outings for that yard and same story on first two starts for this stable.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Delves Hoppings Fillies' Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)
Spotlight: Novice wins on first two starts (7f/1m, AW/good) before keeping on strongly for Listed third at York (1m, good) in May; a sizeable step forward is needed today but the extra yardage could be a firm plus; one to consider.

From a good middle-distance family but there were definite signs of ability over 1m as a 3yo, as on her latest turf start when showing the right spirit in fifth of 11 at Nottingham (first two now have marks in the high 80s); of interest up in trip but entitled to need this belated return.

Sole win in 7f AW maiden as 3yo; very understated in early handicaps for this yard but he's been involved in three tight finishes at 1m3f and 1m2f on AW in 2026 and the step up to 2m in March looked more of an experiment than a plan; acts on turf and has claims back at 1m2f.

Successful in 1m handicap on AW as 2yo but he ran through the options over that trip; seemed to stay this trip on Lingfield AW last time, coming from off the pace to take third of six behind one who followed up and this might end by not being a true test of stamina anyway; respected.

RaceName: Wrexham Lager Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Soundly beaten on first two hurdling starts (2m/2m5f) and not the most obvious answer here.

RaceName: Wrexham Lager Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Won two small-field Irish points this spring (both on soft) and made pretty good start to rules career when third at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good) a fortnight ago; ought to be suited by this move back up in trip; good chance.

RaceName: Wrexham Lager Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Out of an unraced sister to high-class chaser Nacarat; bettered low-key Irish point debut when third of 12 last month (good to yielding), form that was boosted when runner-up won a recent Hexham bumper; subsequently bought for £19,000; interesting to see how she fares on rules debut.

RaceName: Wrexham Lager Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Improved with experience in bumpers, most recently when second to well-backed newcomer at Newton Abbot (good) six weeks ago; that form probably isn't anything to get too excited about but she's likely to be suited by today's longer trip and should have a future over jumps.

RaceName: Wrexham Lager Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Half-sister to useful jumper Johnson's Blue; placed on soft/heavy ground in the last three of her six points, and subsequently changed hands for £10,000; makes rules debut after 138-day break and encounters much quicker conditions but good market support will make her of interest.

RaceName: Wrexham Lager Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Third foal; half-sister to low-grade chase winner Pearl Island; dam 2m6f-3m hurdle winner, sister to 3m-3m5f chase winner Bretney, closely related to useful 2m4f-4m hurdle/chase winner Captain Cattistock; stoutly bred filly; wouldn't need to be anything special to get competitive on debut.

RaceName: Wrexham Lager Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Gave it a good shot from the front when third of five in junior hurdle at Leicester (1m7f, soft) in November but two lesser efforts for Ryan Potter followed; now with new stable (same owners); improvement required.

RaceName: McGuinness Maintenance Services Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Pulled-up in a maiden hurdle in 2024 and well beaten in a Flat maiden on return; cheekpieces on; best watched.

RaceName: McGuinness Maintenance Services Irish EBF Maiden
