Spotlight: Fifth foal; half-brother to winners incl' Special Wan (6.5f-1m inc US turf Grade 3; RPR 118), Yerwanthere (7f/1m inc AW/US; 108) and Over The Blues (Listed-placed 6f; 96); related to some smart types; late starter but worth a market check.

RaceName: McGuinness Maintenance Services Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: The best of three maiden runs was when just denied at Roscommon over an extended 1m2f; keen and might not have stayed 1m5f at Leopardstown since but still a fine effort; tongue-tie added and is a leading player back down in trip.

RaceName: McGuinness Maintenance Services Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: A fine effort on debut at Cork over this trip on soft ground; there should be improvement to come from that and can be a player if as effective on this faster surface; Colin Keane aboard and one of three here for the yard.

RaceName: McGuinness Maintenance Services Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Was only 2.25l behind the classy Causeway in a soft ground maiden over 7f on second start last year; hasn't really built on that though some respectable runs since; rated 82 so shouldn't be far away.

RaceName: McGuinness Maintenance Services Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Was an eyecatcher on debut over C&D last month, finishing just 3/4l behind the re-opposing Saxony Charms; the same terms today so there shouldn't be much between them again and likely to be involved.

RaceName: McGuinness Maintenance Services Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Well beaten in two maidens and is a more likely candidate for handicaps.

RaceName: McGuinness Maintenance Services Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Second foal; dam German Listed-placed 1m3f winner, half-sister to German 1m2f Group 3 winner Sea Of Sands, out of 1m2f-10.7f winning sister to US 8.5f Grade 3 winner Salve Germania; cost E41,000 as a foal; respected connections and a notable jockey booking so market strength should be noted.

RaceName: McGuinness Maintenance Services Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Both starts at Gowran; improved nicely from his debut over 1m when third over 1m1.5f next time; there should be further improvement and can be involved if staying this longer trip.

RaceName: McGuinness Maintenance Services Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Some promise in two runs last year; improved effort on return over this trip at Leopardstown; the tongue-tie worn then is retained and shouldn't be far away with improvement.

RaceName: McGuinness Maintenance Services Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Eleventh foal; half-brother to six winners inc Caponata (9.4f-1m2f inc Listed; RPR 113), Brooch (1m-9.4f inc Group 2; 110) and Raymonda (7f inc 2yo; 107); dam French 5f Listed/5.5f 2yo winner (101); well-bred sort cost 32,000gns as a yearling; interesting to see his market position compared to two stablemates.

RaceName: McGuinness Maintenance Services Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Second foal; dam once-raced sister to Champion Stakes winner Fascinating Rock, closely related to 5f/6f winner Marwari, half-sister to smart 1m4f-2m Flat and 2m hurdle winner Quick Jack; is by a leading sire and from a quality damline; worth checking for market strength on debut.

RaceName: McGuinness Maintenance Services Irish EBF Maiden
OwnerName: Team Valor International LLC & A Oliver
Spotlight: Third runner for yard; well-bred sort ran a fine race on debut over C&D last month, 3/4l in front of the re-opposing Numantia; should have improved since and likely to be a major player.

RaceName: McGuinness Maintenance Services Irish EBF Maiden
Spotlight: Promise on debut over 1m4f at this course but might not have handled soft ground when was poor at Down Royal since; others have stronger credentials.

RaceName: McGuinness Maintenance Services Irish EBF Maiden
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan
Spotlight: Made a pleasing debut when fourth (beaten nearly 7l) over 1m1.5f at Gowran two weeks ago; can improve from that but others still hold stronger form claims.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Bradford Gosforth Park Cup Handicap
Spotlight: Smart on his day and his third at Wolverhampton (6f) last July saw him record an RPR of 115; down the field in two runs for Mick Appleby this year and he now drops to 5f for the first time in his career; tongue tied for the first time.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Bradford Gosforth Park Cup Handicap
Spotlight: Ex-Italian 5yo; made a dream start for Marco Botti when powering home to win a 5f handicap at Southwell in March off just 2lb lower; that hasn't been a springboard to better things though and he could prove vulnerable; blinkers return after one run in cheekpieces.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Bradford Gosforth Park Cup Handicap
Spotlight: He hasn't won since a Listed success at York (6f, soft) in October 2023; in the meantime he has often attracted support, favourite in the 2024 Stewards' Cup and last season's Ayr Gold Cup; two perfectly creditable efforts for Robert Cowell this year and he showed promise in one Tapeta run last spring; not discounted for a trainer who runs four.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Bradford Gosforth Park Cup Handicap
OwnerName: Middleham Park Racing Xii & Mrs G Cowell
Spotlight: Six AW runs have yielded four wins, a second and a close fifth (stable debut after a six-month break and off a 7lb higher mark than today's); has blown hot and cold on turf since April but the blinkers worn for his last four wins now return (has raced exclusively in cheekpieces since joining Robert Cowell) and he should make a bold bid to maintain an unbeaten C&D record.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Bradford Gosforth Park Cup Handicap
Spotlight: On a tough mark after a big-race win in Bahrain in January; no impact in two runs this month and he is due to be 3lb lower from the weekend.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Bradford Gosforth Park Cup Handicap
Spotlight: Won 5f handicaps at Haydock (good) and Hamilton (soft) on his first two runs for Ed Bethell last September; beaten favourite in both starts this year, latterly at York six weeks ago which is a strong piece of form; no obvious reason why he won't handle AW and he is one to take seriously.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Bradford Gosforth Park Cup Handicap
Spotlight: Looked a smart prospect last season, beating his 21 rivals at York's Dante meeting and second of 20 at the Ebor meeting; not at his best in three runs in Dubai but this mark should be in range and he has won on Tapeta and after a break; goes without accessories (usually hooded) today; interesting for a yard in form.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Bradford Gosforth Park Cup Handicap
Spotlight: Ex-Irish 4yo; best run for current stable when ninth at Thirsk last month; the return to 5f/AW won't faze him but this looks a warm race in which to end his losing streak.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Bradford Gosforth Park Cup Handicap
Spotlight: Had some strong handicap form at York last summer; appreciated the return to turf when beating his nine rivals, including the odds-on Corolla Point, at Beverley (5f, good) in April; another solid York run when keeping on for fifth last month but slow starts are creeping in and he may prove vulnerable for win purposes back on AW.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Bradford Gosforth Park Cup Handicap
Spotlight: Capitalised on his drop in the weights when coming from off the pace to win at Goodwood (5f, good) last month; disappointed back at Goodwood (6f, soft) 19 days ago; stable has stronger claims elsewhere.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Bradford Gosforth Park Cup Handicap
Spotlight: Six wins and five seconds from 17 AW runs; unplaced on turf on his last two starts and although this scenario will suit better, a few others look better handicapped.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Bradford Gosforth Park Cup Handicap
Spotlight: Prominent racer; two wins this season, one of them C&D (2-2 here); back near his best when a close third of 17 at Thirsk last month but this is a stronger race.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Bradford Gosforth Park Cup Handicap
OwnerName: M McHale, J Munroe, M Guy & D Walshe
Spotlight: Made giant strides in 2025, winning four times including a big-field 5f Class 2 handicap at Southwell; only 1lb higher today but his 2026 campaign has been up and down and others are more appealing for win purposes.

RaceName: JenningsBet In Bradford Gosforth Park Cup Handicap
Spotlight: Won this race 12 months ago off a 1lb higher mark and recorded his seventh C&D win when scoring in December; solid second on turf three weeks ago; should run his race.

OwnerName: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid
Spotlight: Has led; won 6f Windsor novice as 2yo, also in the frame in Listed events; found little when upped to 7f on Newbury comeback but finished close up back over 6f at Windsor recently in new aids (tongue-tie retained, blinkers discarded); stamina still to be proved over 7f but can't rule out.

All three runs at 6f; beat a subsequent maiden winner in good style on Kempton AW on second start then was awkwardly positioned before finishing a never-nearer runner-up at Windsor (good to firm) in May; failed to enter the stalls since; capable of better off his current mark if seeing out 7f.

Won 6f maiden and novice events as 2yo; two good efforts over 7f this year, doing well when attempting to give weight to a big improver on the other Newmarket course (good) and no disgrace in a Lingfield defeat next time when having to be switched; solid chance.

OwnerName: The Old Stag Racing Sy Partnership
Spotlight: Back in good form in refitted cheepieces this spring, winning twice at Newton Abbot (3m2f, good) before very respectable third at Hexham (2m7f, good); not sure to be ideally suited by today's drop back in trip or the switch back to blinkers but still considered.
Bounced back to form in refitted cheekpieces for Harry Derham last month, winning twice at Warwick (2m5f, good); subsequently changed hands for £12,000; remains well handicapped on older form.

Returned from six-month break with career-best performance in May, winning readily by 5l over C&D (good), and is now 5-13 over hurdles; recent 8b rise asks a question of her but she's evidently in very good nick.

Late-maturing 10yo who won twice last spring and maintained progress with some good runs in defeat in second half of last year; may not have been suited by soft ground when pulled up on latest outing; not out of this.

RaceName: O'Driscolls Irish Whiskey Irish EBF Stayers Handicap
Spotlight: Stayed on well to gain third career success off 8lb lower here on latest (1m6f, yielding); both previous runs at around this trip have not been his best so could be vulnerable upped in distance on quicker ground off career high mark.

RaceName: O'Driscolls Irish Whiskey Irish EBF Stayers Handicap
Spotlight: 1m4f Fairyhouse maiden winner last season (gd-yld); promising seasonal reappearance here 33 days ago, staying on for a 5.5l fourth of 24 over 1m6f (2l behind Kilmeaden in third); well worth a go at this trip on that evidence and mark unchanged; cheekpieces added.

RaceName: O'Driscolls Irish Whiskey Irish EBF Stayers Handicap
Spotlight: Dual-purpose; hurdle winner is 1-9 on the Flat; has come up dry since winning a Galway Festival maiden last July (12.5f, yielding); kept on nicely for third over 1m6f here latest (Delta behind); raised 2lb for that but should go well again up in trip.

RaceName: O'Driscolls Irish Whiskey Irish EBF Stayers Handicap
Spotlight: Dual-purpose; signed off last season with a solid effort in the Cesarewitch at Newmarket (2m2f, gd-fm); may have needed the run over an inadequate 1m6f here last month; sharper now and moves up in trip; beaten in this last year off 8lb lower but respected nonetheless.

RaceName: O'Driscolls Irish Whiskey Irish EBF Stayers Handicap
Spotlight: One of two for yard that has won two of the last four renewals; useful hurdler was well held on return to the Flat behind Poetic Sound here on latest (1m6f, yielding) but was runner up in this last year on good ground off 1lb higher; dangerous to discount.

RaceName: O'Driscolls Irish Whiskey Irish EBF Stayers Handicap
Spotlight: Second runner for yard; sole win on Flat came over 1m on AW in 2023 in France; may have needed the run when weakening over 1m6f here (good) on latest last month; ran well over 18.5f in Ulster Cesarewitch last September off 1lb lower; chance stepped back up in trip.

RaceName: O'Driscolls Irish Whiskey Irish EBF Stayers Handicap
Spotlight: Veteran; talented hurdler a latecomer to the Flat but showed promise in maidens and bolted up on handicap debut in this sphere at Listowel 26 days ago (2m, good); hiked up 9lb for that and this is a deeper race but he can't be discounted.

RaceName: O'Driscolls Irish Whiskey Irish EBF Stayers Handicap
Spotlight: Comfortable 3l win over 1m6f (good) at this venue off 5lb higher last May but wasn't seen again in 2025; soundly beaten in two runs this term at 1m4f/1m6f and needs to revive now upped in trip.

RaceName: O'Driscolls Irish Whiskey Irish EBF Stayers Handicap
Spotlight: Veteran has notched up nine wins over jumps but is 1-9 on the Flat and hasn't won in this sphere since 2024; however, he was a close third in this last season off 1lb higher on good ground so he's not dismissed; 1lb wrong.

RaceName: O'Driscolls Irish Whiskey Irish EBF Stayers Handicap
OwnerName: Sarah Dale & Wayne Dale & Ian Foster
Spotlight: Dual-purpose; 3-13 on the Flat for Michael Bell, all three wins over 1m6f on fast ground; has placed AW form at around this trip; modest form on easy ground over hurdles so far for this yard but return to quicker conditions on the Flat could revive; 2lb wrong.

RaceName: O'Driscolls Irish Whiskey Irish EBF Stayers Handicap
Spotlight: Three-time winner at 1m4f-1m7f on testing ground in France; ground may be an issue on first start for new stable; 3lb wrong and best watched in new headgear combination.

RaceName: O'Driscolls Irish Whiskey Irish EBF Stayers Handicap
Spotlight: Showed promise in maidens but first three handicap runs have been pretty underwhelming; beaten 9l at Fairyhouse on latest (1m6f, good); 6lb wrong here; others appeal more.
His two standout efforts for this yard have come over 6f at Kempton; dropping into a Class 5 can help; each-way shout.
Now 4lb lower than when winning at Southwell (5f) in March; creditable efforts on turf more recently and he now drops into a Class 5; has a much better chance than his form figures might indicate.

OwnerName: D Scott, Resdev, Capla Dev & L Westwood
Spotlight: C&D winner; runner-up in two of his three starts this year; edging up the weights but he should give it a good go.
Can blow the start but he won two C&D handicaps in late 2025 and is well treated on that form; trainer has won the last two renewals of this race; high on the list.

OwnerName: New Vision Bloodstock & Steel A Win
Spotlight: Tumbling down the weights and one to be interested in when the stable emerges from the doldrums.
Two wins from three AW starts (including C&D) as opposed to 0-11 on turf; has a higher mark in this sphere as a result but it is probably still in range; one to note for market confidence.
Placed in all three runs this year, racing wide when third of nine at Beverley (5f, good) ten days ago; trainer, who also saddles Tiriac, has won the last two runnings of this race.

Two wins in 5f Class 5 handicaps in 2026, at this track in February and then when beating Fierce at Carlisle three weeks ago; stable form is a plus but he needs to bounce back from a quiet run at Hamilton.

OwnerName: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde
Spotlight: Reeled off a hat-trick last summer (5f-6f, turf and AW) and went close to landing a four-timer when second at Bath in August; below form this term (unable to live up to his market position on last two starts) and he is due to be 2lb lower from the weekend.

OwnerName: N Irving, F Steele & I Jardine
Spotlight: Three wins in 2026, the first two over C&D; another career-best display when scoring at Ayr last month and her two defeats since were in Class 3 and 4 company; considered.

0-11 but she has performed with credit in each of her four 6f runs since undergoing wind surgery last November; close third off this mark at Hamilton last week; drops to 5f for the first time, with cheekpieces enlisted; each-way shout.
Course winner (6f); arrives having been in good form, seeing off her 11 rivals at Thirsk (5f, good) three weeks ago; a 4lb rise shouldn't prevent another solid effort.
Recorded his seventh AW win when landing a 5f handicap (0-60) at Wolverhampton in April; ran as well as he ever has on turf when third of six at Ayr 37 days ago; needs more in this company.

Dual 1m2f AW winner in 2024; upped from 1m4f to 2m under Alicia Perkins on Kempton AW 16 days ago and gave a positive response, as she travelled smoothly in rear but met some traffic before running on into second of eight; has some turf form (not this year) and could have more to offer as a stayer.

Ex-French 4yo who is 1-7 for current connections, easily best of those outings all from off the pace over 2m on Kempton AW; five-day break was blamed when he tailed off on turf (2m, good to firm) on latest outing, but that still leaves him with something to prove.

OwnerName: Ursa Major Ltd & Hicky Parmar Racing
Spotlight: Four-time hurdles winner whose first Flat success came on Southwell AW (1m6f) in January; has kept his form pretty well, including over 2m, and is considered seriously in this small field despite having had just two races (2025) on Flat turf.

Ran away with a Southwell handicap (1m6f, AW) last October; second twice there (2m/1m6f) this April and he wasn't beaten far when making the running on good to firm at Yarmouth (1m6f) eight days ago, albeit coming last in a three-runner race; chance if he takes to the first-time headgear.

10yo whose last three wins came in this race (2022, 2023 and 2025) and it's now named after him; did it in good style from the back last year (good to firm) and is now 3lb higher; made a satisfactory return to action at Yarmouth last month and the chief concern today is probably whether there will be enough pace in the race for his stamina to kick in sufficiently.

RaceName: Rachel Jones Memorial Novices' Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Fairly useful on the Flat when based with Ed Walker; well beaten on stable/hurdle debut in January but his next two runs were much more encouraging, and there is still hope that more of his Flat ability can be transferred to this discipline.

RaceName: Rachel Jones Memorial Novices' Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: In the frame all five hurdling starts, most recently when second in Cartmel novice (2m1f, good) last month, after a break; ought to be very competitive again on this handicap debut.

RaceName: Rachel Jones Memorial Novices' Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Kept on for third of 13 in Hereford maiden (2m, good) in April but was below that form when well-held third of six over that C&D on last month's handicap debut; needs help from the new tongue-tie here.

RaceName: Rachel Jones Memorial Novices' Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 2-2 since switched to handicaps, the latter win gained at Southwell (2m, good) this month; did that with quite a bit more in hand than 1l-margin might suggest and could still be well treated after latest 8lb rise.
Won twice over hurdles last winter but was below form in that sphere when last seen.
Triple point winner was an easy hunter chase winner last time; this trip might be a bit sharp.
Killarney maiden winner over 1m last year but was well beaten on return over that trip.

OwnerName: Mrs Sheila O'Ryan & John F Doyle & Grant Leon
Spotlight: Bumper, hurdle and Flat winner ran a solid race on return at Listowel and is a big player.
Point, hurdle and chase winner was poor over hurdles last time and has to rebound.
AW Flat winner was disappointing over hurdles when last seen over a year ago.
Well beaten over 1m on return at Limerick last time and has a bit to find here.
Only two of 10 wins have been on turf but rated 80 so has to be respected in this grade.
Hurdle and chase winner ran respectably on sole Flat start; not a bad hurdle run last time; chance.
Has shown ability without winning on the Flat and over hurdles; poor hurdle run last time, though.
Hurdle winner ran a solid race at Downpatrick last time; has a bit to find on Flat debut.
Easy Roscommon winner last year and a solid fifth at Gowran last time; best form on soft.
Kilbeggan h'cap hurdle winner last year was pulled-up next time; has a bit to find on return.

Not beaten far when fourth here (1m) in January and sixth here (1m2f) in March; mid-division at Doncaster (1m2f) later in March when last seen; drops back in trip after a break and is probably best watched.

Of interest on his good third at Dundalk (5f, Polytrack) in January on final Irish start; fairly encouraging fifth on stable debut at Ayr (5f, good to firm) last month; couldn't get into it at Southwell (6f, Tapeta) last time and he's just 1-17, but is not written off.

His last seven wins have come over 7f and the return to 6f isn't an obvious plus, but he's run well over 7f/1m at Carlisle on his two starts this month and has won here three times; might not be far away.

Displayed improved form in first-time cheekpieces to get off the mark at the eighth attempt at Carlisle (6f, good) last Monday; a 5lb penalty may not stop this 4yo if the headgear maintains its effect.

OwnerName: The Jocks And The Geordies II
Spotlight: Inconsistent 5yo whose losing run goes back 27 races and he was below par at Wetherby last week, however he was a close second at Ripon (6f, good) prior to that and is effective here; each-way claims if back on song.

Just one win from his 20 starts but that came over C&D, and Tapeta brings out the best in him; not disgraced on last month's reappearance at Bath and he's down to a handy mark; of interest now back here.

Unplaced elsewhere on final three starts for Ben Haslam but wasn't running badly and three out of his last four wins have come at this venue; on a career-low mark and could play a leading role back here in a first-time tongue-tie on his stable debut.
Returned to form when third at Ripon (6f, good) last month and again ran well when second at Ayr (7f, soft) last Saturday; his sole win came here and he could be involved.
Won a classified race at Kempton (7f, Polytrack) in March but he hasn't come up to scratch on his five starts since and needs to raise his game.

Course winner who was fourth of 13 in this last year and was a close second at Catterick last month; however, this inconsistent 9yo has been soundly beaten on his last two starts and has to leave those runs behind.

0-12 and has failed to threaten on either start this year but when fifth of 14 at Beverley (7.4f, good) last Tuesday he was trapped wide and failed to settle; his best run last year came when 2l fourth at Wolverhampton (6f, Tapeta) in May off 8lb higher; the return to a sprint trip may bear fruit.

Remains a maiden after 15 starts but she ran well when third in a first-time hood in a classified at Nottingham (6f, soft) a fortnight ago; unexposed over sprint trips and in the headgear, and she's not ruled out.

Won off 4lb higher here (5f) last November; she's failed to get competitive subsequently but has a nice mark to work with if the first-time visor (replacing cheekpieces) prompts a revival; watch the betting.
0-12 and hasn't achieved much; this 5yo needs a career-best if she's to win today on her stable and seasonal debut.

Building up a solid record for this yard, including one win on AW (this February) and two at Windsor (May and June); the latest win was in tidy style over almost this far 11 days ago and, although not well-in under his penalty, he has major claims again.

Made hay initially for this yard last March-June; pretty consistent this year but with only the one win (with first-time visor over hurdles in April) and he may again have to settle for minor honours.

Third start for new yard when winning from the front at Wolverhampton (1m4f amateurs, AW) four weeks ago on latest outing; back up 4lb; chief concern is that it's over two years since he raced on firmer than good and he was well beaten on last four attempts on it; last July's turf win was on good to soft.
0-14 but achieved some minor honours over 1m-1m4f on AW this year and another when staying-on fourth of nine at Bath (1m2f, good) on latest start; arguably has a bit to prove over this far.

Broke her duck at first attempt for this yard, in a 1m4f classified in February; returned to turf with a creditable third in handicap at Windsor (11.5f, good to firm) before winning one comfortably from midfield at Bath (11.5f, good) 13 days ago; up 5lb but she's now on a bit of a roll.

RaceName: Tom Jones Live At Chester Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won two claiming hurdes for Archie Watson (1m7f/2m) last spring but was 0-6 in handicaps for current stable afterwards; absent since heavy defeat over 2m3f in November and this reappearance run is his first venture beyond 2m5f; not the percentage call.

RaceName: Tom Jones Live At Chester Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Hinted at possible return to form when fourth at Stratford (2m6f, good to firm) last month and is now 2lb lower than for last summer's win over that C&D but he was soundly beaten at Market Rasen (2m4f) a fortnight ago and now has questions to answer again.

RaceName: Tom Jones Live At Chester Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Suffered alarming dip in form after 2m3f course win (good to soft) last April but ran well from the front and was seemingly back in good nick when third over that trip here (good) last month; contender if able to build on that effort over this new trip.

RaceName: Tom Jones Live At Chester Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Gained all three hurdle wins for Stuart Edmonds over 3m on heavy and soft ground at Southwell, the latest in February; changed hands for 2,400gns in April and now makes stable debut after 104-day break; tongue tied for first time; may find the ground too lively.

RaceName: Tom Jones Live At Chester Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Out of form during light campaign last season but cashed in on much-reduced mark at Southwell (2m4f, good) this month; not yet proven over this longer trip but recent 4lb rise ought to be manageable; respected.

RaceName: Tom Jones Live At Chester Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Seemed to respond well to first-time cheekpieces when second in a small-field point in April but has not raced under rules since 70l defeat on handicap hurdle debut in November 2024 (2m7f); has tongue-tie refitted here; others have more persuasive profiles.

RaceName: Tom Jones Live At Chester Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-6 over hurdles but looked suited by step up to 3m when second under Megan Bevan at Ludlow (good; made last-flight error) last month, form that was recently given a good boost by the third; high on the list.

RaceName: Tom Jones Live At Chester Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Ran quite well when third of seven (albeit well held in the end) on recent chasing debut at Hexham (3m, good) but is now 0-9 under all codes; 2lb wrong for this return to hurding; needs to find something extra.
